scope_summary: Problem: Generator and iterator protocol semantics in Mamba are not verified against CPython 3.12. `yield`/`yield from`/`send`/`throw`/`close`/`StopIteration.value` and async generators (`async for`, `asend`, `athrow`, `aclose`) need conformance testing; the iterator protocol (`__iter__`/`__next__`/`StopIteration`) needs verification on user-defined classes and itertools-style iteration. Issue #756 — independent of the perf rewrite (#1187). Success Criteria: R1-R5 (P0) covering core protocol, send, throw, close, and yield from delegation; R6 (P1) iterator protocol; R7 (P1) async generators; R8 (P2) edge cases. AC1-AC8 give the concrete acceptance checks. All ported from CPython 3.12 Lib/test/test_generators.py with a commit-hash header comment, plus mamba-specific regression tests where cranelift codegen paths differ. Boundary: In scope — crates/cclab-mamba/src/runtime/generator.rs (semantic layer, 280 LOC per runtime/generator.md), crates/cclab-mamba/src/runtime/async_generator.rs if it exists as a separate file, crates/cclab-mamba/tests/conformance/generators/ (new ported tests). Out of scope — #1187 generator perf rewrite (this issue is the conformance gate that rewrite must pass), asyncio task wrapping (only the async generator protocol itself), contextlib.contextmanager conformance (separate stdlib issue). No contradictions found: existing runtime/generator.md R1-R5 align with issue R1-R5 (state machine, yield/suspend, iterator protocol, send, throw/close). runtime/iter.md covers the iterator protocol side. runtime/async.md / runtime/closure.md describe the suspension machinery. testing/mamba-py312-conformance.md and testing/conformance.md describe the conformance test harness pattern this change will follow. No naming/format/error-handling conflicts between the issue's proposed approach and the existing specs. Non-conformant behaviors discovered during test authoring will be fixed in the same change per the user's Tests+Fix decision in pre-clarifications.
